title: NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit
exclusions: Does not read mailbox bodies, does not prove directors opened attachments, and does not decide whether a postponed report legally satisfies national transposition of NIS2 — counsel remains accountable for those judgments.
evidence: Weekly `UC-22.2.42` JSON/CSV export with `evidence_id`, underlying `_audit` query scoped to `savedsearch_name`, and the signed timestamp metadata referenced in `evidenceSigning` for chain-of-custody.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.20 requires management bodies to approve and oversee cyber risk measures. The UC audits the distribution and acknowledgement of board-level cyber risk reports. It proves reports were delivered and read-receipted but cannot assess whether the board's deliberation was substantively adequate, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.20 (Governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.42: NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.42' running on index _audit and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture: lookup row report_name="NIS2_Board_Risk_Monthly" quarterly frequency; `_audit` contains no `action=search` events whose `savedsearch_name` equals that string in the last two quarters — weekly run returns `missed_cadence=1`, populates `evidence_id`, and lists the stale `distribution_list` for remediation.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fixture sanity check geared toward non-alerts: `_audit` includes a fresh `action=search` hit 18 days ago for the same saved search with `info` mentioning `sendemail`, while the lookup still expects `monthly` cadence — `days_since` stays 18, below the **35-day** monthly tolerance, so the outer `where` clause removes the row and the alert remains silent which auditors treat as the desired healthy state.

dataSources: `index=_audit` with `sourcetype=audittrail` (Splunk internal audit trail for scheduled searches) carrying `action`, `info`, `search_id`, `user`, and `savedsearch_name` when the scheduler renders PDFs and invokes `sendemail`. Expected `info` strings include renderer hints and mail-transport markers. Coverage expectations and board distribution lists are versioned in `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` (`report_name`, `frequency`, `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date`). Platform context: Splunk Cloud always retains `_audit`; Splunk Enterprise installs should mirror this visibility with admin roles — see [Splunkbase 361](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/361) packaging for on-prem Enterprise.
app: Splunk Enterprise 9.x ([Splunkbase 361](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/361)) or Splunk Cloud Platform — no extra TA required because `_audit` ships with the deployment.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_board_report_schedule.csv
| eval report_key=lower(report_name)
| join type=left report_key [
    search index=_audit sourcetype=audittrail action=search earliest=-180d
    | where match(info,"(?i)sendemail|pdf|rendered")
    | eval report_key=lower(savedsearch_name)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_delivery by report_key
]
| eval expected_cadence_days=case(frequency="monthly",35,frequency="quarterly",100,frequency="weekly",10,1=1,14)
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_delivery), round((now()-last_delivery)/86400,0), null())
| eval missed_cadence=if(isnull(last_delivery),1,if(days_since>expected_cadence_days,1,0))
| where missed_cadence=1
| eval evidence_id=md5(report_name . "|" . frequency . "|" . if(isnotnull(last_delivery), tostring(last_delivery), "no_audit_hit"))
| table report_name, frequency, distribution_list, last_expected_date, last_delivery, days_since, expected_cadence_days, evidence_id
description: Reconciles each `report_name` from `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` against `index=_audit` scheduler events and highlights board cyber packs whose PDF/email dispatch is absent or older than the policy cadence encoded in `frequency`.
value: Gives the board secretary and incident leadership an automatic paper trail that scheduled cyber risk briefings actually left Splunk on time, which matters when regulators ask whether oversight artefacts reached directors—not merely whether dashboards exist.
implementation: (1) Keep `savedsearch_name` identical to `report_name` (case-insensitive) or maintain a mapping lookup. (2) Ensure alert actions log `sendemail` with `format=pdf` where possible so `info` matches the SPL predicate. (3) Delegate ownership to Head of IR with secretariat co-ownership documented in the runbook. (4) Weekly schedule with **180-day** lookback. (5) Archive positive outcomes to `index=audit_evidence` if your programme requires WORM storage beyond `_audit` retention.
visualization: Table of missed rows with `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date`, and `evidence_id`; timeline of `last_delivery`; single value count of breaches by `frequency`.

references.title: Splunk Docs — About auditing Splunk activity
Splunk Docs — Schedule reports in Splunk Web

knownFalsePositives:
• **Executive read-only SharePoint drop instead of email** — Distinguish: board portal ingestion skipped `sendemail` but files landed in Microsoft 365; `info` lacks mail tokens. Suppress: add `alternate_delivery=sharepoint` to `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` and `lookup` it to bypass email predicates when flagged.
• **Saved search renamed mid-quarter** — Distinguish: `_audit` shows a new `savedsearch_name` while the lookup still lists the legacy `report_name`. Suppress: maintain `nis2_report_name_alias.csv` and `lookup` before the join to normalise keys.
• **High-availability failover reran the same job twice** — Distinguish: duplicate `search_id` values within seconds with identical `user`. Suppress: keep `| stats latest(_time)` as written; optionally `dedup search_id` inside the subsearch.
• **Quarterly blackout where the CIO postponed the pack during M&A quiet period** — Distinguish: formal board communication logged in the exception register. Suppress: time-bound exception rows in `nis2_reporting_exceptions.csv` with `quiet_until` evaluated via `inputlookup` in a preliminary `eval`.
• **PDF renderer succeeded but email action disabled in `savedsearches.conf`** — Distinguish: `info` references `rendered_report` without `sendemail`. Suppress: extend the `where match(info,...)` clause or add a correlated `index=_internal` sourcetype=scheduler filter after Change Approval documents the configuration drift.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110, Exchange Online message trace)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, board meeting task tracking)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (REST API for scheduled report audit)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `_audit` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_board_report_schedule.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.20`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.20 (Governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.42: NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `_audit` under `audittrail`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Keep `savedsearch_name` identical to `report_name` (case-insensitive) or maintain a mapping lookup. The lookup table identified here is a long-lived compliance artifact; version it in source control and track every change with a pull-request review.

• **Hint 2:** Ensure alert actions log `sendemail` with `format=pdf` where possible so `info` matches the SPL predicate. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Delegate ownership to Head of IR with secretariat co-ownership documented in the runbook. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** Weekly schedule with **180-day** lookback. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** Archive positive outcomes to `index=audit_evidence` if your programme requires WORM storage beyond `_audit` retention. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of NIS2 control activity.

This search correlates the outer dataset (index=`_audit`, sourcetype=`audittrail`) with a subsearch on `report_key` across the last 180 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.42: NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -180d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.42,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=_audit sourcetype=audittrail earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `report_name`, `frequency`, `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date`, `last_delivery`, `days_since`, `expected_cadence_days`, `evidence_id`. Run:

```spl
index=_audit sourcetype=audittrail earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table report_name, frequency, distribution_list, last_expected_date, last_delivery, days_since, expected_cadence_days, evidence_id
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.42: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Of Missed Rows With `Distribution List` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — `Last Expected Date` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — And `Evidence Id`; Timeline Of `Last Delivery`; Single Value Count Of Breaches By `Frequency` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We keep a dated log of whether the big cyber summary that is meant for directors was actually sent on schedule, so nobody has to guess if the board saw the risk picture on time.
